runtime_proof.note: Pure TypeScript contract enforcement — expiration boundary semantics are deterministic from timestamp comparison alone. No live DB required. isDualAuthExpired() and isApprovalExpired() are stateless and replay-safe.
queries: EXP-1a: isDualAuthExpired(pending, expiresAt) === true (exact boundary is expired)
EXP-1c: both contracts return identical boundary verdict (normalized)
EXP-2: isDualAuthExpired at exact boundary === true (fail-closed)
EXP-2b: isApprovalExpired at exact boundary === true (fail-closed)
EXP-5: replayApprovalChain at boundary throws DualAuthViolationError (replay-consistent)
EXP-5b: computeExpiresAt is deterministic — same inputs produce same expiresAt
EXP-6a: completeApproval at boundary throws DualAuthViolationError
EXP-6b: completeApproval 1ms before boundary succeeds, returns frozen ApprovalRecord
EXP-6c: assertApprovalNotExpired at boundary throws ApprovalExpiredError

row_counts.note: No database tables modified — dual-auth expiry boundary normalization is pure TypeScript contract enforcement with no DB dependency. Expiration semantics are deterministic from timestamp comparison alone.
